issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text on white background
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa. When rendered on the default white background (#ffffff), this produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 82: --colour-text-secondary: #767676; /* or darker to achieve 4.5:1 on white */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users across all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 390: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible on screen instead of hiding it for screen readers only. This fails to provide a proper visually-hidden utility and may expose content intended only for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418-428: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (line 570), and the .btn:active state uses transform: translateY(1px) (line 591). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform animation for users who prefer reduced motion, which may cause vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 421-423: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transform: none !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } }

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, clip: auto, and visible overflow, which keeps the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while exposing it to assistive technology. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only behavior.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for hero CTA button

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only an outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring for keyboard users in standard mode. Assistive technology users navigating with keyboards cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class uses transition: transform for its slide-in animation without a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable the motion, which may cause issues for users with vestibular disorders who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator for high-contrast mode users who rely on visible focus rings.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) and makes it impossible for keyboard-only users to track their location.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 232: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content and may remove it from the accessibility tree inconsistently. This breaks skip links and screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color ..., transform ...), causing movement animations that are not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size: .btn min-height: 32px

issuesFound.fix: Line 558: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the default state. This relies solely on colour to identify links, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 267: text-decoration: underline; OR add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to the a selector.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block for :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing focus indicators in high-contrast mode where they are critical.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~256: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The universal :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~246: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px WCAG 2.5.8 minimum. The padding of 6px (space-1-5) is also below the 8px threshold, resulting in an effective touch target of only 32px.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~420: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~218: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset, which renders nothing when outline is zero. No box-shadow or border replacement is defined in the paired base .btn rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~361: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible. It also lacks proper clip properties, meaning screen reader text will be visible on screen.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~273: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, falling in the 24–43px range without the recommended 8px padding to reach 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~521: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. While padding is 10px, the explicit min-dimensions fail the 24px requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body text uses color: var(--colour-text-primary) which resolves to #555555 (foreground) on background-color: var(--colour-bg-page) which is #ffffff. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.74:1, which fails the WCAG 2.2 requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 223: Change --colour-text-primary to a darker value such as #333333 (ratio 12.6:1) or ensure --colour-neutral-900 is set to at least #767676 (ratio 4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but does not provide a replacement focus indicator (no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is declared in this rule). Keyboard users cannot see when buttons have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 576: Add a visible focus indicator such as box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); or outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the .btn:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision, making it difficult to distinguish between words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or change to word-spacing: normal; to ensure adequate spacing between words.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule. Without an outline width and style, no focus ring is rendered. Keyboard users will not see focus on elements using this utility class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: Add outline: 2px solid; to the .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible rule-block (the color is already defined).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override that disables this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially triggering vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: Add a rule inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 469 to disable the transform transition: .btn { transition: none; } or remove transform from the transition property.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 649: Change min-height: 20px; to min-height: 24px; (or increase padding to ensure the clickable area is at least 24px tall).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover, border-bottom, or font-weight change) to distinguish links from surrounding text. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not recognize these as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 832: Add text-decoration: underline; on hover or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to provide a non-colour visual indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users will not see when this button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 709: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); to the .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating a touch target smaller than the 24px minimum. This makes activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663: Change min-width: 24px; and min-height: 24px; to meet the minimum target size requirement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links set outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating dropdown items will not see focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 783: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; to the [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users will not see when this button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); to the #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating to the logo link will not see a focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 682: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); to the .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 836: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1067: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or border: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the .feature-card a:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see which filter button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the .filter-tabs button:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see focus on these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1388: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when checkboxes have focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select these small controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1626: Change width: 24px; and height: 24px; to meet the minimum target size requirement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the .footer-brand a:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see when these expandable buttons have focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1644: Add outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule-block.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and lacks compensating spacing of at least 8px. Users may have difficulty activating this small button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 816: Increase width: 44px; and height: 44px; or add padding: 8px; to meet the recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no explicit padding, resulting in a touch target of exactly 24px (the minimum) but below the 44px recommended size without compensating spacing. This may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1641: Add padding-block: 10px; or increase min-height: 44px; to meet the recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform (transition: transform var(--transition-slower), ...) that creates spatial movement on hover. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation, which may cause issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 947: Add a rule inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transition: .hero-image img { transition: none; }.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards have a transition on transform (transition: ... transform var(--transition-base)) that moves the card on hover. This animation is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially causing accessibility issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1033: Add a rule inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transform: .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base); } (removing transform).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The arrow pseudo-element after feature card links has a transition on transform (transition: transform var(--transition-fast)) that animates movement on hover. This is not overridden in the prefers-reduced-motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1058: Add a rule inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transition: .feature-card a::after { transition: none; }.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards have a transition on transform (transition: ... transform var(--transition-base)) that moves the card on hover. This animation is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1228: Add a rule inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transform: .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); }.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards have a transition on transform (transition: ... transform var(--transition-base)) that causes spatial movement on hover. No reduced-motion override exists for this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1408: Add a rule inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transform: .pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); }.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion arrow has a transition on transform (transition: transform var(--transition-base), ...) that rotates the indicator. While the media query at line 1583 sets transition: none for this element, it only applies when the user prefers reduced motion. However, the empty media query at line 469 does not cover this specific selector, and the animation should be explicitly disabled there or the transform transition removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1576: Add .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; } inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 469.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Primary buttons use background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (white #ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and the 3:1 requirement for large text. Users cannot clearly see the button text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: Darken the background color to at least #005a9c (ratio 4.5:1) or use a darker text color on the existing background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Secondary buttons use color: var(--colour-text-primary) (#555555) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (white). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.74:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement. Users will have difficulty reading the button labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 597: Change the text color to at least #767676 or ensure --colour-text-primary provides adequate contrast against white backgrounds.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .badge--brand
issuesFound.explanation: Brand badges use color: var(--colour-info-dark) (#1e40af) on background-color: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.2:1, which is below the 4.5:1 required for the small text (12px) used in badges.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896: Darken the text color to at least #003087 or use a darker background to achieve 4.5:1 contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .badge--success
issuesFound.explanation: Success badges use color: var(--colour-success-dark) (#065f46) on background-color: var(--colour-success-light) (#d1fae5). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for the small badge text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: Darken the text color to at least #004d3a or adjust the background color to achieve adequate contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .badge--warning
issuesFound.explanation: Warning badges use color: var(--colour-warning-dark) (#92400e) on background-color: var(--colour-warning-light) (#fef3c7). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, which is insufficient for the small text size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 904: Darken the text color to at least #6b3000 or adjust the background to meet the 4.5:1 contrast requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .badge--error
issuesFound.explanation: Error badges use color: var(--colour-error-dark) (#991b1b) on background-color: var(--colour-error-light) (#fee2e2). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.7:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for small badge text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: Darken the text color to at least #7a0000 or adjust the background color to achieve 4.5:1 contrast.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform transition without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transform transition without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform transition. The existing media query override at Line 933 is empty and does not disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 933: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .steps-list > .step + .step::before

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby linking to the error message, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on this attribute to convey the invalid state. The paired markValid() correctly removes the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1283: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the drawer and sets internal state, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will still report the menu as collapsed even when it is open. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetrical state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_lockScroll();` and before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap initialization and activation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). Consequently, when the mobile navigation opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, and keyboard users can tab behind the modal overlay. Additionally, open() fails to activate the trap.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1928 (in init()): Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger, escapeDeactivates: true });` after `_initialised = true;`
  Line 1962 (in open()): Insert `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state. The _closeInstance() function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2057: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the active CSS class on filter tab buttons but does not synchronize the aria-pressed attribute. Since these buttons act as toggle buttons (showing a pressed state when active), screen readers cannot convey which filter is active without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2885: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function animates the panel open and updates internal state, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). This leaves screen reader users unaware that the accordion item has expanded. The close() function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3429: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error state not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error, so users may not be notified of the validation failure.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and announces the state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the trigger in a contradictory state where it appears open visually but reports closed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function updates aria-expanded="false" but does not announce the state change via the live region. Users relying on screen readers will not be notified when the navigation menu closes, causing disorientation.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. This creates a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility tree state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function updates aria-expanded="false" but does not announce the state change via the live region. Users relying on screen readers will not be notified when a dropdown closes.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the panel and announces the state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the trigger in a contradictory state for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function updates aria-expanded="false" but does not announce the state change via the live region. Users relying on screen readers will not be notified when an answer collapses.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips._onChipClick function removes a filter and re-renders the list but does not announce the change via the live region. Users relying on screen readers will not know which filter was removed.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner appearance not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function displays the dialog and traps focus but does not announce its appearance via the live region. Users relying on screen readers may not realize a dialog has appeared if focus management is delayed.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button aria-pressed not initialized if reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init function only calls play() if reduced motion is not preferred. If reduced motion is active, play() is skipped, leaving the play button without an initial aria-pressed state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table collapse action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded function announces when the table expands but fails to announce when it collapses. Users relying on screen readers will not be notified when the comparison table is hidden.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, even though the error message is associated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1024: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid() function, before the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Only the _closeInstance() function sets it to "false". This leaves screen readers unaware when the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1316: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance() function body.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel and updates CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Only the close() function sets it to "false". This prevents screen reader users from knowing which items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2884: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open function adds visual classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state desynchronization where screen reader users are not informed the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1599: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel to show it but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", leading to inconsistent accessible state reporting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1442: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function expands the answer panel visually but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. Since the close function sets aria-expanded="false", screen reader users will not receive the correct state update when opening an item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1841: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the error message element via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field itself. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1387: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onAccept function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept function grants analytics consent and hides the banner but does not announce this significant status change to screen readers via a live region. Users may be unaware that tracking has been enabled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3825: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onDecline function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onDecline function revokes analytics consent and hides the banner without announcing the status change to screen readers. Users may be unaware that tracking has been disabled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3833: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');

fpTitles: dropdownMenu _openInstance function missing aria-expanded update
faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies cannot convey the invalid state to users without this attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function displays the mobile navigation drawer by adding CSS classes but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button from "false" to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands an FAQ answer panel and animates its height but neglects to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is now visible.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus updates DOM visibility but does not announce the state change via the liveRegion utility. Users relying on screen readers are not informed when the menu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance: Add after setAttr:
```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
```
Line _closeInstance: Add before emit:
```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');
```

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing Enter/Space keyboard handling
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard handler supports Arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation between FAQ triggers, but does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the accordion panel. When the trigger is a non-button element (e.g., a div with role="button"), keyboard users cannot activate it.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function (Line 1605) toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding classes and removing the hidden attribute, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). The corresponding close function (Line 1635) correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1605: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function (Line 1826) reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element (inst.trigger). The close function (Line 1838) correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving assistive technology unaware that the dropdown is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1826: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function (Line 2635) expands the answer panel by removing the hidden attribute and animating height, but it omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger (item.trigger). The close function (Line 2663) correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing a discrepancy in the accessibility tree state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2635: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function (Line 1395) is used to mark form fields as invalid. It links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Consequently, screen readers are not explicitly notified that the field contains an error, relying solely on the description link which may be missed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel visibility toggles do not manage aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module uses the hidden attribute to show/hide panels (Line 1826 open, Line 1838 close) without updating aria-hidden. Explicitly setting aria-hidden="false" when showing and aria-hidden="true" when hiding ensures the accessibility tree is correctly updated across all user agents.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1826: inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1838: inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm error message assigns function source code instead of string
issuesFound.explanation: In the newsletterForm._onSubmit function (Line 3075), if the error status is not 409, the code assigns _showError.toString() to the msg variable. This results in the function's source code being announced to screen readers and displayed to users instead of a meaningful error message, violating SC 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3075: const msg = err.status === 409 ? (STRINGS.newsletterDuplicate || 'You're already subscribed with this email.') : (STRINGS.newsletterError || 'Subscription failed. Please try again.');

issuesFound.title: contactForm submit handler does not focus first invalid field
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm._onSubmit function (Line 2997) scrolls the first invalid field into view when validation fails but does not move keyboard focus to it. Users navigating by keyboard or screen readers may lose context and not know which field needs correction, degrading the form error recovery experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2997: if (firstInvalidField) { scrollIntoView(firstInvalidField); firstInvalidField.focus(); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions (Lines 1826, 1838) change visible content by showing or hiding menus but do not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown opens or closes, unlike other components in this file (e.g., mobileNav, faqAccordion) which do announce state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1826: _openInstance: ... liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1838: _closeInstance: ... liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap is never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module creates a focus trap instance (_trap) but never calls _trap.activate() within the open function (Line 1605). Consequently, focus is not confined to the navigation drawer when it opens, allowing keyboard users to tab out of the modal overlay into the underlying page content, violating focus management requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1605: _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function omits aria-expanded update

issuesFound.fix: Line ~1890: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets CSS classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (note: the close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the state is never announced as open).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3550: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer and removes CSS classes, but it never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1910: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the prices update visually across plan cards, but no aria-live region announcement informs screen reader users that prices have changed or what the new prices are.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2860: After updating all plan cards, insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to ' + period + ' billing');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button stored in `inst.trigger`.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2040: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the function after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Product filter results missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge is updated, but screen reader users are not informed via a live region that filtering completed or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2480: Inside the `filterAnimation.run` callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to clear aria-invalid on valid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1395: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after updating the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2055: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the function before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The init function finds the trigger button but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for the closed state on page load, leaving the attribute missing until interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1942: After `_initialised = true;`, insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, the function does not move keyboard focus into the drawer (e.g., to the first focusable element or the drawer itself), leaving keyboard users stranded at the trigger button while the rest of the page is obscured.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1895: After opening the drawer, insert `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, causing keyboard users to lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1915: Before the closing brace, insert `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid attribute management
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, the code updates CSS classes and aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. When fields become valid, it does not clear aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot identify which fields have validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2694: In the `if (result.valid)` block, after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);` add:
    `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`
    In the `else` block (invalid), after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);` add:
    `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manage the panel visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button to reflect the current state. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1534: After `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` add:
    `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
    Line ~1549: At the start of the function (before the early return check) add:
    `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen reader users to inform them of the state change. This is particularly problematic when the menu opens programmatically or via keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1534: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);` add:
    `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
    Line ~1549: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);` add:
    `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() and close() functions animate the panel height and manage CSS classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified when accordion panels expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~3128: After `item.open = true;` add:
    `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
    Line ~3152: After `item.open = false;` add:
    `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually with animation, but there is no announcement to screen reader users informing them of the change or the new pricing information.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2871: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });` add:
    `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing' : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as a tab list where one button is always active, but the code only toggles CSS classes and does not update aria-selected (or aria-pressed) to communicate the active state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2445: Inside the forEach callback, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add:
    `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation declares a focus trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open navigation drawer into background page content, violating modal dialog focus containment requirements.

Line ~1470: After `_initialised = true;` add:
    `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
    Line ~1414: In the open function, after `_lockScroll();` add:
    `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens an FAQ panel and sets item.open = true, but unlike the close() function which sets aria-expanded="false", the open() function never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not set aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (role="combobox") but when the suggestion list opens, aria-expanded is not set to "true" on the input element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the _open() function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not set aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the suggestion list, the combobox input's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2460: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the beginning of the _close() function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox input is initialized without aria-expanded="false", leaving screen readers unaware of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` in the mount/setup phase, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the function removes error styling and aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3610: In the if branch (valid case), after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Although the functions accept an announceToSR parameter, they never use it to announce the expand/collapse state changes via the liveRegion utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: In open(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' expanded');`. In close(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Despite accepting an announceToSR parameter, the functions do not announce navigation open/close state changes to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: In open(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. In close(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll to top button is activated, the page scrolls and focus moves, but the action completion is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: After `setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }, 300);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen readers to indicate the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1720: In _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. In _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` before the setTimeout.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons registered via `dropdownMenu` are toggled by `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` which update visibility but do not update `aria-expanded`. The `register()` function also fails to initialize `aria-expanded`. Screen reader users are not informed of the dropdown state.

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) acts as a combobox trigger. The `mount()`, `_open()`, and `_close()` functions manage the suggestion list visibility but do not update `aria-expanded` on the input element. Screen reader users cannot detect when suggestions are visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands the answer panel and updates CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3094: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed or aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) toggle active state visually via CSS class in `_syncTabAriaStates()`, but do not update `aria-pressed` or `aria-selected`. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2593: function _syncTabAriaStates() { ... setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); ... }

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` and `srUtils.markValid()` functions manage error descriptions but do not set or remove `aria-invalid="true"` on the form fields. Screen reader users are not notified that the field value is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1273: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1285: function markValid(field, errorEl) { ... field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID); ... }

issuesFound.title: Product count badge updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters()` function updates the product count badge text via `_updateCountBadge()`, but does not announce this change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the number of visible products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2555: function _updateCountBadge(count) { ... liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} shown`); ... }

issuesFound.title: Price display updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod()` function updates price text content across all plan cards when the billing period changes, but does not announce this change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2773: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); ... }

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function only updates aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. The markValid function removes aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving fields incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1523: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); Line 1534: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); (or removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID))

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap and aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer (users can tab outside), and focus is not returned to the trigger button when closing. The drawer element also lacks aria-modal="true", causing screen readers to not treat it as a modal dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1987: Add const trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); trap.activate(); Line 2003: Add trap.deactivate(); and _trigger.focus(); Line 2019: Add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.MODAL, 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the UI has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1987: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen); Line 2003: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update their aria-expanded attribute when panels open or close. The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The _closeInstance function does not set aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2156: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 2169: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing dropdown menus is not announced to screen readers, preventing users from knowing when a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2156: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened); Line 2169: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they do not initialize aria-expanded="false" to reflect the initially collapsed state of the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2204: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not reflecting aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the code adds error classes and calls srUtils.markInvalid but does not explicitly set aria-invalid="true". When validation passes, it does not clear aria-invalid. This prevents screen readers from identifying field validity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3105: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); Line 3110: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); (or removeAttr)

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ items updates the aria-expanded attribute but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3547: Add liveRegion.announce(item.title + ' ' + STRINGS.expanded); Line 3563: Add liveRegion.announce(item.title + ' ' + STRINGS.collapsed);

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated result count (e.g., "Showing 5 products") is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2964: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterResults.replace('{count}', totalMatches));

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods changes visible prices but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3234: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged.replace('{period}', period));

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the color theme (light/dark/system) is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the theme has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4284: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.themeChanged + ' ' + label);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu focus not returned to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown closes (especially via Escape key), the returnFocus parameter is accepted but not used to return focus to the trigger button, leaving focus in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2169: Add if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus(); at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-describedby for errors
issuesFound.explanation: When a file upload fails, the error message is announced via liveRegion but the upload zone itself does not reference the error message via aria-describedby, preventing users from associating the error with the field when navigating back to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3716: Add srUtils.addDescribedBy(binding.zoneEl, statusEl); when error occurs.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step changes missing aria-live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While the wizard does announce step changes via liveRegion.announce, it does not clear the live region before announcing, potentially causing duplicate announcements in some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3476: Add liveRegion.clear(); before liveRegion.announce();

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation missing reduced motion check
issuesFound.explanation: The stat counter animation checks SUPPORTS.reducedMotion (which is a frozen value at script parse time) rather than checking the live media query with prefersReducedMotion(), potentially animating for users who changed their preference after page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4124: Change if (SUPPORTS.reducedMotion) to if (prefersReducedMotion()).

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the main content or top of page, though it does return focus appropriately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2334: Add setAttr(_btn, 'aria-controls', 'main-content'); (assuming main-content exists).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not manage aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()`, `mobileNav.close()`, and `mobileNav.init()` functions toggle the navigation drawer's visibility via CSS classes but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users are not informed whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer focus trap not activated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function creates a focus trap instance (`_trap`) but fails to call `_trap.activate()` when the drawer opens. Keyboard focus is not constrained to the drawer, allowing users to tab into the obscured page content behind the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2378: if (_trap) _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox does not manage aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount()`, `show()`, and `hide()` functions manage the suggestion listbox visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the search input (combobox). Screen reader users are not informed when the suggestion list is open.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._syncTabAriaStates()` function toggles the `is-active` CSS class on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3653: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function sets `aria-expanded="false"` in the close function and initialization, but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` when opening an item. Screen reader users are told the answer is collapsed even when it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4695: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid upon focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the error description but fails to remove the `aria-invalid` attribute (or set it to "false"). Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1705: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animator updates content without aria-live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `statCounterAnimator._animate()` function dynamically updates the `textContent` of stat elements to count up numbers, but the elements lack an `aria-live="polite"` attribute. Screen reader users are not informed of the changing values.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6125: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid

issuesFound.fix: Line 2825 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element it controls. This prevents assistive technologies from understanding the relationship between the trigger and the controlled panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3142 (in init()): After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters and updating the visible product cards, there is no announcement to screen readers about the change in result count (e.g., "Showing 5 products"). Users may not realize the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4090 (in applyFilters, inside the filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown panels are opened or closed, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated. The panel visibility changes via classList and hidden attribute, but the trigger state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3425 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open'...`. Line 3440 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close'...`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates visually but no announcement is made to screen readers about the change or the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4260 (in setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);` after `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);` in init, or in setPeriod itself after updating the prices.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are opened or closed via the public methods (including toggle), the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated, even though it is set during initialization. This causes a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility tree state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4525 (in open()): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`. Line 4545 (in close()): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu opens or closes, no status message is announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3156 (in open()): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open'...`. Line 3176 (in close()): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close'...`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the function removes aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845 (in markValid): Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden via CSS class removal, but if the CSS uses off-screen positioning (transform: translateX) rather than display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true", allowing screen reader users to navigate to invisible content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3170 (in close()): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 3155 (in open()): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: Although a focus trap variable is declared, it is never initialized with createFocusTrap() or activated when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer when open, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3143 (in init()): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`. Line 3156 (in open()): Add `_trap.activate();`. Line 3175 (in close()): Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called (currently conditional on `isActive()` but _trap is always null).

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top but no announcement is made to screen readers to confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3325 (in _onClick): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' });`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
