issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button toggles the visibility of the FAQ answer panel but does not communicate its state via aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot tell if the panel is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" ...>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the error message element is never rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.error} to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as a toggle group (single selection via `activeFilter` state) but lack `aria-pressed`. Screen reader users cannot identify which category is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 933: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle Monthly button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period button acts as a toggle (state tracked by `value` prop) but lacks `aria-pressed`. Screen reader users cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle Annual button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period button acts as a toggle (state tracked by `value` prop) but lacks `aria-pressed`. Screen reader users cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1142: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

fpTitles: BillingToggle Annual button missing aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid on the underlying input element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of form fields using this component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 670: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides missing aria-hidden for non-visible slides
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slides that are not currently visible are not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users will hear all testimonial content at once instead of only the currently displayed slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1720: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup using incorrect error prop name
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed a prop named error, but the component expects errorMessage. This causes the error message not to render, and the aria-describedby on the input references a non-existent element ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error">

issuesFound.title: Disclosure toggle trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button in NavDropdown controls the visibility of a submenu via the isOpen prop but does not expose this state to assistive technology. Screen reader users will not know whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Form input missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field but does not map this prop to the aria-invalid attribute on the underlying input element. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid when errors occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Carousel slides missing aria-hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive testimonial slides are visually hidden via CSS classes but remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users will hear all slides instead of just the current one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

fpTitles: Form input missing aria-invalid attribute mapping

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup` to indicate it opens a menu, but it lacks `aria-expanded` to communicate whether the menu is currently open or closed. Screen reader users cannot tell if the submenu is visible without this state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 862: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button element.
  Line 862: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons indicate the active filter via CSS classes, but they do not communicate this selected state to assistive technology. Since these buttons act as a toggle group where one is always pressed, they need `aria-pressed` to indicate which category is currently active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1274: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element.
  Line 1274: `<button type="button" className={[ 'filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The Monthly and Annual billing toggle buttons show the selected state visually but do not expose it to assistive technology. As toggle buttons, they require `aria-pressed` to indicate which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1417: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}` to the first button.
  Line 1417: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`
  Line 1425: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}` to the second button.
  Line 1425: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to style the field when it contains an error, but it does not pass the `aria-invalid` attribute to the underlying `<input>` element. Screen readers will not announce that the field is invalid even when the visual styling changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 783: Add `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` to the input element props.
  Line 783: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has `aria-controls` linking to the answer panel, but it is missing `aria-expanded` to indicate whether the panel is currently open or collapsed. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the state of the disclosure.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1584: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button element.
  Line 1584: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button for the background video toggles state but relies only on changing aria-label text. It should use aria-pressed to programmatically indicate the on/off state to assistive technology.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle button manages the isOpen state for the navigation panel but does not expose this state via aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot tell if the menu is open or closed.

'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The submenu trigger button in the mobile navigation uses isExpanded state (derived from openSectionId) to show/hide child links but lacks aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot announce the submenu state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1256: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element in SkipLinks does not have an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page, users cannot distinguish this skip navigation from other navigation regions.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous slide button is an IconButton containing only the "‹" character but the required aria-label prop is not provided. Screen readers will announce only the raw character or nothing at all.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1796: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" (or similar): <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection <section> element lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby. As a major page region, it needs an accessible name to help screen reader users identify and navigate to this landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1293: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading": <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field error message not linked via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but the FormGroup component is not passed errorId="newsletter-email-error", so no element with that ID is rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" to FormGroup: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Contact form message field error message not linked via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea uses aria-describedby="contact-message-error" but FormGroup does not receive errorId="contact-message-error". The error message element lacks the expected ID, breaking the association for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: Add errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Statistics bar section landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button group missing aria-label

issuesFound.title: Hero trust badges group missing aria-label

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation dialog missing aria-modal

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component receives an `invalid` prop for styling but does not map it to `aria-invalid` on the underlying `<textarea>` element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: `<Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error ? 'true' : undefined} aria-describedby={[counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} />`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea references `contact-message-error` via `aria-describedby` (conditionally), but the FormGroup wrapper does not receive the `errorId` prop, so the element does not exist in the DOM when errors occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1675: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-message-error">`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing aria-busy during submission
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button shows a loading spinner via the `loading` prop but lacks `aria-busy="true"`, so screen-reader users are not informed that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1700: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog element missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `role="dialog"` lacks the `aria-modal="true"` attribute, which is necessary to indicate that the dialog is modal and that the content beneath it is inert. Additionally, the `aria-modal` attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1805: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has incorrect aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-modal="true"` attribute is placed on the overlay `<div>` rather than on the dialog element itself. This may cause screen readers to incorrectly identify the modal boundary or fail to recognize the dialog as modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>` (remove aria-modal="true" from this line)

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button changes aria-label text but lacks aria-expanded={isOpen}. Screen reader users rely on aria-expanded to know the menu state without listening to the full label change.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles a nested submenu but does not have aria-expanded to reflect the isExpanded state. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the state change.

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Individual star spans are decorative within a container that already has an aria-label. Without aria-hidden, screen readers announce each star character redundantly after the label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362: <span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>

issuesFound.title: Button inline spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner span is purely decorative when isLoading is true, as the text announcement is handled by VisuallyHidden. Without aria-hidden, it may be announced as empty content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: <span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component renders an emoji as its only content but no aria-label is passed. Icon-only buttons require an accessible name to be usable by screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The close button contains only an '✕' character with no aria-label passed to the IconButton. Screen reader users will not understand the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 913: <IconButton
  onClick={onClose}
  aria-label="Close navigation menu"
  className="mobile-nav__close"
>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel Prev IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button is icon-only ('‹') with no aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify it as a navigation control.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel Next IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button is icon-only ('›') with no aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify it as a navigation control.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter button uses visual styling to indicate active state but lacks aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}. Screen reader users cannot perceive which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The link uses a visual class for the current page but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed that this link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The link uses a visual class for the current page but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed that this link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: This is a navigation landmark but lacks an accessible name. If there are multiple navs on the page, users cannot distinguish this one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: This is a primary navigation landmark but lacks an accessible name. Users cannot distinguish it from other nav regions like the footer or mobile nav.
issuesFound.fix: Line 863: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav inner nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: This navigation region inside the mobile panel lacks an accessible name. It should be distinguished from other navs on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 919: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: This major page section lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 739: <section
  aria-labelledby="hero-heading"
  className="hero-section"
>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The section contains a heading #stats-heading but does not reference it via aria-labelledby. The landmark lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 798: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The article element contains a heading but does not reference it via aria-labelledby. Screen readers navigating by article/region cannot identify the plan name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1256: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing aria-label with plan name
issuesFound.explanation: The link text is generic relative to the context (e.g., "Start Pro free trial"). Sweep TSX-J requires the aria-label to explicitly include the item name for clarity in lists.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk is not hidden from assistive technology. While "(required)" is visually hidden text, the asterisk itself should be aria-hidden to prevent redundancy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 508: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button disables interaction but does not set aria-busy="true". Screen readers announce the button as disabled but not necessarily that content is loading.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: This list is a visual duplicate for animation looping purposes. Without aria-hidden, screen readers will announce the integration names twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1167: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">
